My daughter, she's the one who brought me here from Life Care, she says this all started this morning, but I feel like I've been here forever. My right arm—it just won't listen to me, it's like a dead weight, and my hand is just useless, I can't even hold my water cup. And I'm trying to talk to you, but the words, they get stuck in my throat, even though I know exactly what you're saying to me, I just can't say it back! I’ve been having this weird drainage issue, kind of like when I get those infections down there, you know, the urinary ones? I’ve been dealing with that for a while. Oh, and Dr. H was here already, I think? Or maybe that was somewhere else? They took some pictures of my head, or maybe they’re going to, I don't know, everything is a blur. I don't have a headache, and I can breathe just fine, but my mouth feels so dry. I take so many pills, the Lortab and the insulin and the ones for my heart and my mood—my daughter has the list, it's a whole pile of papers. I don't smoke or drink, I haven't done that in years, but my arm is still just hanging there and I’m really scared. Did I tell you about the pain? Well, not really pain, just… everything feels heavy on this side. Am I going to be okay? It’s just so frustrating not being able to get the words out.
